title: Sticking With It
notes:
This album does a great job of blending some classic soul, complete with horns and harrowing vocals, and a modern twist. A je ne sais quoi. This album sets the Dip as a pioneer in the Neo-soul genre for me, along with the likes of Thee Sacred Souls and St Paul. 
 Highlights are the great swing, better harmonies, and simplicity. Tracks like 'Anyways' and 'Eye of the Storm' are perenial favorties of mine when I want to listen to somber soul sounds.

 Lowlights are not really apparent. I think this album only lacks a bit of all around diversity to elevate it to a 9 or 10.
